kings-langley.localInfo: Kings Langley's convenient location means residents can easily drop off devices on their commute. We service the full range of Apple Macs, from vintage models to the latest releases.
kings-langley.titleDriver: Tough Mac Repairs for Working Pros
kings-langley.introParagraph: We repair at the component level — corroded charge controllers, cracked solder joints from vibration, and dust-packed thermal systems. Tradespeople and commuters need devices that survive real-world punishment, and we rebuild them to last.

faqs.q: My MacBook is full of plaster dust — can you clean it?
faqs.a: Yes. We do thorough internal cleaning including fans, heatsinks, and board surfaces. This prevents overheating and extends your Mac's lifespan significantly.

faqs.q: Can I drop off before work and collect after?
faqs.a: That's how most of our customers do it. Most repairs finish same-day, ready for evening collection.

faqs.q: Is it worth repairing my 2016 MacBook Pro?
faqs.a: Usually yes. An SSD and battery refresh gives it years more life at a fraction of replacement cost. We'll advise honestly if it's not.

kings-langley.ctaText: Get a no-nonsense diagnostic and honest quote — built for people who work hard.

faqs.a: Never. We recommend the most cost-effective path. If a repair makes sense, we'll do it. If it doesn't, we'll explain why honestly.

faqs.q: How much does a basic MacBook battery replacement cost?
faqs.a: Costs vary by model but we always quote upfront. No hidden fees — the price you're quoted is the price you pay.

faqs.q: Can you speed up my old iMac cheaply?
faqs.a: Yes. An SSD upgrade is the single most impactful and affordable improvement. Most older iMacs run like new afterwards.
